channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine-US talks on ending war with Russia start in Saudi Arabia as Kyiv launches huge drone attack
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior officials from Ukraine and the United States opened talks Tuesday on how to end Moscow’s three-year war against Kyiv, hours after Russian air defenses shot down 343 Ukrainian drones in the biggest such attack since the Kremlin ordered the full-scale invasion of its
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Tyson Fury and Wayne Rooney to lead England squad at Soccer Aid 2025
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Former world heavyweight boxing champion Tyson Fury and ex-Manchester United forward Wayne Rooney will coach an England squad made up of athletes and celebrities for Soccer Aid 2025 at Old Trafford.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope Francis follows Vatican spiritual retreat as doctors say he's no longer in imminent danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis participated remotely in the Vatican's spiritual retreat Tuesday after getting good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the double pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How the White House hired Republican political firms to launch an anti-migrant ad campaign
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security skipped a fully competitive bidding process to give two Republican-linked firms the first part of a $200 million television ad campaign that lauds President Donald Trump for his crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dems accused of threatening government shutdown over Musk hatred and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans are marching ahead with a government funding bill despite Democratic opposition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans will face a critical test of their unity when a spending bill that would avoid a partial government shutdown and keep federal agencies funded through September comes up for a vote. Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles County said Monday that he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez because the brothers have repeatedly lied about why they killed their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida house collapses with 2 people inside during tornado
item.description: The storm knocked out power and took down trees in the area, as well as dealing minor damage to some nearby structures.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is signaling that the Trump administration has finished its six-week purge of programs of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know ahead of the talks between the US and Ukraine in Saudi Arabia
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Saudi Arabia is to host talks on Tuesday between the United States and Ukraine in a new diplomatic push after an argument erupted during President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's Feb. 28 visit to the White House.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drawing huge crowds, Bernie Sanders steps into leadership of the anti-Trump resistance
item.description: WARREN, Mich. (AP) — Bernie Sanders is standing alone on the back of a pickup truck shouting into a bullhorn. He's facing several hundred ecstatic voters huddled outside a suburban Detroit high school — the group that did not fit inside the high school's gym or two overflow rooms.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, Fox 5

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy released after Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Andreas Kronthaler honors Westwood with a theatrical, rule-breaking vision at Paris Fashion Week
item.description: At Vivienne Westwood’s fall show Saturday, Andreas Kronthaler sent a clear message: rebellion is alive and well. The collection, staged at the Pavillon Cambon, was a riot of irreverence and refinement, merging razor-sharp tailoring with Westwood’s signature sense of mischief.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, ABC 4

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, Fox 5

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and spotlighting a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4
